title: The Day the Sky Fell in Siberia
story: BOOM! A massive explosion rocked Siberia in 1908, flattening trees for miles around. On June 30, a giant fireball lit up the sky near the Stony Tunguska River. It remains the largest impact event ever recorded in human history.

After years under Belgian rule, these two neighboring countries finally took control of their own destinies. Celebrations erupted in the streets as people danced, sang, and waved their new national flags. It was a day of joy and hope for a brighter future.
activity_question: What two countries gained independence in this story?
follow_up_question: Why do you think independence was important to the people of Rwanda and Burundi?

title: The Clock That Needed an Extra Second
story: Tick tock, tick tock... wait, add one more tick! On June 30, 1972, clocks around the world got a bonus second. Scientists added this tiny extra time, called a leap second, to keep our clocks in sync with Earth's slightly slowing rotation. It's like giving time itself a quick breather to catch up!
activity_question: What did scientists add to clocks in 1972?
bonus_fact: Leap seconds were also added on June 30 in 1981, 1982, 1983, and 1985.

title: DNA Pioneer Born on June 30
story: A future DNA detective entered the world on June 30, 1926. Paul Berg was born in New York City, destined to unravel genetic mysteries. This brilliant biochemist would go on to develop groundbreaking techniques for studying and modifying DNA. His work was so important that in 1980, Berg received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his contributions to understanding the building blocks of life.
activity_question: What prize did Paul Berg receive for his work?
follow_up_question: Can you think of ways studying DNA might help solve problems in the world?

title: Space Dreams Take Flight
story: A future space explorer took his first breath on June 30, 1951. Stephen S. Oswald was born in Seattle, Washington, destined for the stars. He grew up to become a NASA astronaut, soaring into orbit on three exciting space shuttle missions. Oswald's adventures in space inspired countless kids to reach for their own dreams.
activity_question: What did Stephen S. Oswald become?
follow_up_question: How many space shuttle missions did Oswald fly on?

title: A Vietnamese Space Pioneer
story: Bùi Thanh Liêm, born on June 30, 1949, in Hanoi, Vietnam, dreamed of touching the stars. He grew up to become Vietnam's first cosmonaut, bravely venturing into the vast unknown of space. Liêm's journey inspired a generation of young Vietnamese to look up at the night sky and imagine the possibilities beyond Earth's atmosphere.
activity_question: Where was Bùi Thanh Liêm born?
follow_up_question: What do you think it would be like to be the first person from your country to go to space?

title: Climbing Pioneer Born: Tom Frost
story: Tom Frost, a legendary rock climber, entered the world on June 30, 1936. Born in Los Angeles, California, Frost would go on to scale new heights in more ways than one. He not only conquered challenging cliffs but also founded Patagonia, a famous outdoor gear and apparel company that adventurers love today.
activity_question: What company did Tom Frost found?
follow_up_question: Why do you think a rock climber would start a company that makes outdoor gear?
